title: Tubular isolation valve resettable lock open mechanism
abstract: A safety valve features a flow tube operated flapper for the normal open and closed positions that can be obtained with one or two control lines to a principal operating piston. Pressure applied to the piston moves the flow tube to rotate the flapper open behind the flow tube. Release of pressure to the principal piston allows a closure spring to return the flow tube up to let the flapper close. A secondary piston can drive the flow tube with applied pressure through a control line. Cycling the applied pressure in combination with an indexing mechanism allows the flapper to be locked open and then released to normal operation. The pistons act as backup for each other as they both drive the flow tube. The flow tube has a clearance fit to the body in the locked open position to exclude debris from the flapper.
claims: Claims We claim: 1. An isolation valve assembly for a tubular string, comprising: a housing having a passage therethrough and a flapper (10) selectively actuated by a flow tube (6) axially movable in said passage by an actuation assembly (2,3) located outside said flow tube (6) and at least in part in said housing; characterized by wherein movement of said actuation assembly (2,3) is regulated by an indexing assembly (48,50,52) causing rotation of said actuation assembly (2,3) as said actuation assembly (2,3) translates in opposed directions such that said flow tube (6) can be selectively locked (56,58) to said housing by said actuation assembly (2,3) with said flapper (10) in an open position, said actuation assembly (2,3) releasing said flow tube (6) due to rotation, to allow said flapper (10) to thereafter be closed and opened with said actuation assembly (2,3). 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein: said actuation assembly (2,3) responsive to a first pressure application and reduction cycle applied to said actuation assembly (2,3) for said selective locking (56,58) and further responsive to a second pressure application and reduction cycle applied to said actuation assembly (2,3) for said allowing said flapper (10) to open and close. 3. The assembly of claim 2, wherein: said actuation assembly (2,3) comprises a piston (3) engaged to said flow tube (6) for tandem movement therewith in a first direction responsive to pressure application in said first pressure application and reduction cycle. 4. The assembly of claim 3, wherein: said flow tube (6) and said piston (3) moving in tandem in a second direction opposite said first direction responsive to a return spring acting on said flow tube (6) and a reduction of pressure to said piston to complete said first pressure application and reduction cycle. 5. The assembly of claim 3, wherein: said piston comprises a detent (56) selectively rotating to engage said housing (58) at a conclusion of said first pressure application and reduction cycle. 6. The assembly of claim 5, wherein: said detent (56) selectively rotating to a disengaged position from said housing (58) at a conclusion of said second pressure application and reduction cycle. 7. The assembly of claim 5, wherein: said piston operably connected to said indexing assembly (48,50,52) that further comprises a j-slot pattern (48) interacting with a pin (50) . 8. The assembly of claim 7, wherein: said detent comprising said pin (50) interacting with ends of slots defining said j-slot pattern (48) during said first and second cycles of pressure application. 9. The assembly of claim 7, wherein: said pin (50) does not interact with ends of slots defining said j-slot pattern (48) during said first and second cycles of pressure application. 10. The assembly of claim 9, wherein: said pin (50) is stationary and supported by said housing and said slots of said j-slot pattern (48) are disposed on said piston for translation and rotation relative to said pin (50). 11. The assembly of claim 9, wherein: said j-slot pattern (48) has alternating short and long slots (68,52) such that said flow tube (6) is held against said flapper (10) when said pin (50) is in a said short slot and said flow tube (6) can move away from said flapper (10) when said pin (50) is in a long said slot. 12. The assembly of claim 11, wherein: said piston comprising a detent (56) that engages said housing (58) when said pin (50) is in a said short slot (68); said detent (56) is rotated to avoid said housing (58) when said pin (50) is in a said longer slot (52). 13. The assembly of claim 7, further comprising: a primary piston (3) connected to said flow tube (6) for tandem movement responsive to pressure application and reduction cycles to a primary piston connection on said housing. 14. The assembly of claim 13, wherein: said piston communicating to a piston connection in said housing, whereupon application of said first pressure application and reduction cycle to said piston connection moves said flow tube (6) to hold said flapper (10) open and application and reduction of pressure to said primary piston connection moves said flow tube (6) in opposed directions to open and close said flapper (10). 15. The assembly of claim 14, further comprising: a return spring to move said flow tube (6) in response to reduction of pressure at said piston connection and at said primary piston connection. 16. The assembly of claim 6, wherein: said flapper (10) is held open with said pressure application of said second pressure application and reduction cycle and said flapper (10) closes on said reduction of pressure for any reason in said second pressure application and reduction cycle. 17. The assembly of claim 13, wherein: said piston and said primary piston are rod pistons. 18. An isolation valve assembly for a tubular string, comprising: a housing having a passage therethrough and a flapper (10) selectively actuated by a flow tube (6) axially movable in said passage by an actuation assembly (2,3) located outside said flow tube (6) and at least in part in said housing; said actuation assembly (2,3) releasably locking said flow tube (6) with said flapper (10) in said open position with rotation of at least a part of said actuation assembly (2,3). 19. The assembly of claim 18, wherein: the entirety of said actuation assembly (2,3) rotates while translating. 20. The assembly of claim 18, wherein: at least part of said actuation assembly (2,3) translates without rotation. 21. The assembly of claim 20, wherein: said actuation assembly (2,3) comprises an upper piston (3a) operably connected to a lower piston (3b) for tandem axial movement while enabling said lower piston to relatively rotate with respect to said upper piston. 22. The assembly of claim 21, wherein: rotation of said lower piston engages and disengages a detent on said lower piston to said housing. 23. The assembly of claim 22, wherein: said lower piston is engaged to an indexing assembly (48,50,52) for rotation of said lower piston as said upper and lower pistons move axially in tandem. 24. The assembly of claim 23, wherein: said upper piston further comprises a seal (46) that experiences no relative rotational movement due to exclusive axial motion of said upper piston.
